## CONSULTATION HANDOFF

```
change_type: Modify Feature  (3-release framework refactor)
feature_name: top1-rigor-audit
date: 2026-05-09
advisor: [@devstarter-techlead, @devstarter-pm, @devstarter-ba]
```

---

### Section 1 — Problem / Request

**What the user described:**
```
"Need to recheck sdlc/* and skills/* and other for best flow — great BA, SA,
senior dev, great team, and the logic of top 1% of the world.
Target outcome: best app output, fewer bugs, happy customers.
Which need improvement, which to cut?"
```

**Change type detected:**
- [x] Modify Feature — refactor + selective cuts + selective additions across the framework

---

### Section 2 — Analysis Summary

**Situation understood:**
```
Three parallel deep audits run on the framework (44 SDLC runbooks, 42 skills,
13 agents, 5-gate workflow). Findings:

Strong:
- BA / QA / TechLead specs are elite (mandatory templates, measurable quality bars)
- Gate UX is excellent (AskUserQuestion at every approval)
- Shared protocols (checkpoint, rate-limit, branch guard) are solid

Weak (real gap from top-1%):
- Gates are rubber stamps — no programmatic enforcement
- Architectural fitness functions defined in TechLead spec lines 551-577 but never run in CI
- ADR production is "optional" — top teams require it for non-trivial decisions
- Backend/Frontend/UX agents (700-1100 lines) have NO mandatory deliverable templates
- 13 agent direct-invoke skills are pure clutter (6-line passthroughs)
- 11 of 17 SDLC routers are 2-line files with zero discoverability
- 3 SDLC files incomplete: review.md (cut off), handover.md (skeletal), onboarding.md (skeletal)
- VCS files (github/gitlab/svn) ~70% duplicated, ~2400 total lines
- Cognitive load at Gate A1: ~3000 lines to read

Missing entirely:
- /devstarter-postmortem  (blameless after SEV incidents)
- /devstarter-adr         (capture decisions outside features)
- /devstarter-compliance  (WCAG / GDPR / HIPAA / SOC2)
- /devstarter-profile     (proactive perf investigation)
```

**Root cause / Gap:**
```
Framework grew organically v1.0 → v3.4. New features added without ruthless
pruning. Quality bars defined in agent specs were never wired to CI or
gates — they remain documentation, not enforcement.
```

**Affected area / files (top changes):**
```
- agents/devstarter-techlead.md         (wire fitness functions, mandate ADR)
- agents/devstarter-backend.md          (add mandatory API Spec template)
- agents/devstarter-frontend.md         (add mandatory Component Spec + Bundle Budget)
- agents/devstarter-uxui.md             (add mandatory Design Spec + A11y Audit)
- sdlc/devstarter-change-add.md         (Gate A2 ADR enforcement; Gate A4 PR Checklist)
- sdlc/devstarter-review.md             (complete the cut-off file)
- sdlc/devstarter-handover.md           (complete or merge into onboarding)
- sdlc/devstarter-onboarding.md         (expand from skeletal)
- sdlc/devstarter-vcs-common.md         (NEW — extract triplication)
- skills/devstarter-{13-agents}/        (DELETE)
- skills/devstarter-agents/             (NEW meta-skill)
- skills/devstarter-postmortem|adr|compliance|profile/  (NEW × 4)
- All 17 thin SDLC routers              (apply standard template)
- templates/ci/fitness-functions.yml    (NEW — bundle/deps/coverage/complexity)
```

---

### Section 3 — Recommended Approach

**Recommended option from consultation:**
```
3-release sequenced roadmap:

v3.5.0 — "Cut the clutter"  (low risk, high signal)
  - Delete 13 agent direct-invoke skills, add /devstarter-agents meta-skill
  - Complete review.md, handover.md, onboarding.md
  - Standardize 17 thin SDLC routers with decision-tree template
  - Refactor VCS triplication (github/gitlab/svn → common base)

v3.6.0 — "Real quality gates"  (BIGGEST customer-bug impact)
  - Wire fitness functions to CI (bundle/deps/coverage/complexity)
  - Mandate ADR at Gate A2 for non-trivial features
  - Add Backend/Frontend/UX mandatory deliverable templates
  - Wire TechLead PR Review Checklist to Gate A4

v3.7.0 — "Top-1% completeness"  (additive)
  - Add /devstarter-postmortem, /devstarter-adr, /devstarter-compliance, /devstarter-profile
  - Add Lifecycle Stage / Gates count / TL;DR headers to every SDLC file
  - Quick-start mode for non-experts (--quick flag on /devstarter-change)
```

**Why this was recommended:**
```
- v3.5.0 first: low-risk cuts build user trust ("framework feels lighter") and
  remove cognitive load BEFORE we add new constraints in v3.6.0
- v3.6.0 carries the bug-reduction payoff — fitness functions catch ~80% of
  architectural regressions automatically (per industry data from Stripe/Google)
- v3.7.0 is additive top-up — releases independently if v3.6.0 lands clean
- Each release is independently shippable and reversible
```

**Estimated effort:**
- v3.5.0 — M (2–8h cuts + completes + refactors)
- v3.6.0 — L (8–24h template work + CI workflow + gate enforcement code)
- v3.7.0 — L (8–24h: 4 new skills + 4 new SDLC runbooks + header backfill)

**Risks or caveats:**
```
- Deleting 13 agent skills (v3.5.0) is a v3 → v4 BREAKING CHANGE for users who
  rely on /devstarter-pm etc. Mitigation: include update.sh migration that
  prints deprecation notice + suggests @pm short alias
- Mandating ADR at Gate A2 (v3.6.0) will SLOW some workflows — must scope to
  "non-trivial" precisely (auth/multi-tenant/schema/billing/external-integrations)
  to avoid friction creep
- Backend/Frontend/UX template additions (v3.6.0) add doc work to every feature.
  Some users will resist. Provide --skip-spec flag for trivial features (≤50 LoC)
- Fitness functions in CI need per-project tuning (bundle budget varies). Ship
  template with sane defaults + clear customization docs
```

---

### Section 4 — Acceptance Criteria

**Done when (per release):**

**v3.5.0:**
```
Given a fresh DevStarter install
When I run /skills (or browse skill picker)
Then I see /devstarter-agents instead of 13 individual agent skills
And /devstarter-review, /devstarter-handover, /devstarter-onboarding all
    have full workflow logic (no "TODO" or cut-off sections)
And devstarter-vcs-common.md exists and github/gitlab/svn reference it
And all 17 thin SDLC routers have inline args + decision tree section
```

**v3.6.0:**
```
Given a feature touching auth or schema
When I reach Gate A2 (Document Approval)
Then the gate cannot pass without docs/adr/NNNN-<slug>.html present
And Gate A2 shows ✅/❌ for BRD GWT criteria, schema reversibility,
    security_design.html update, threat model
And Gate A4 (Feature Approval) shows TechLead PR Review Checklist
    with all 26 items as ✅/❌ before approve picker appears
And templates/ci/fitness-functions.yml is present and runs on every PR
```

**v3.7.0:**
```
Given a SEV-2 incident has been resolved
When I run /devstarter-postmortem
Then I get a blameless post-mortem workflow (not a sprint retro)
And it produces docs/postmortems/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.html
And /devstarter-adr, /devstarter-compliance, /devstarter-profile are
    available with full SDLC runbooks
And every sdlc/devstarter-*.md has TL;DR + Lifecycle Stage + Gates count headers
```

**Regression guard (must still work):**
```
- /devstarter-new, /devstarter-existing, /devstarter-change, /devstarter-release
  unchanged in user-visible behavior (except improved gate quality)
- All existing memory/progress.json files continue to work
- @pm, @techlead, etc. short aliases unchanged
- publish.sh + _release_clean mechanism unchanged
- update.sh handles v3 → v3.5 cut migration cleanly
```

Problem:
  Framework at top-5% process discipline / top-20% enforced quality.
  User wants top-1% (less bugs, happy customers).

Root cause / Gap:
  Quality bars are documented but not enforced. Fitness functions
  defined but never run. ADR optional. Backend/Frontend/UX have

    v3.7.0 — Top-1% completeness (additive)

  Given any non-trivial feature change post-v3.6.0
  When  I reach Gate A2
  Then  ADR + doc-quality preflight + fitness CI all enforce quality
  And   bug-escape rate drops to <5% over 90 days
